mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in retail sales signals improving household demand and rising expected corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the trend is measured from currently released RSAFS data.
evidence_refs: fact:September 2010 RSAFS snapshot was 367670 million dollars
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than the unconditional hit rate over a 3-year walk-forward window, or if the return spread versus negative RSAFS momentum is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 9.5%, persistent labor-market slack signals weak wage income and fragile demand, causing analysts to cut near-term earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have a positive 20-day return frequency above 60% or if average triggered 20-day returns are not below non-triggered returns in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25% indicates an active zero-rate policy stance that lowers discount rates and supports risk-taking, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary easing regime
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS below 0.25% is associated with lower average 60-day S&P 500 returns than periods above 0.25%, or if the triggered hit rate is below 50% in out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in industrial production and payroll employment indicate broad real-activity acceleration rather than inventory-only rebuilding, leading to higher expected earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshots showed INDPRO 93.2093 and PAYEMS 130201 thousand
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive INDPRO and PAYEMS condition does not produce higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than either single positive condition alone, or if its walk-forward return spread is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 650 thousand units signal a depressed residential investment channel and weak household balance-sheet repair, reducing confidence in the recovery and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2010 HOUST snapshot was 598 thousand units
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST below 650 is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered cases or if average triggered returns exceed the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices while the federal funds rate remains below 0.25% creates an inflation-scare configuration in which real policy rates fall and future tightening risk rises, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:September 2010 CPIAUCSL snapshot was 218.372 and FEDFUNDS was 0.19 percent
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL acceleration and low FEDFUNDS condition has nonnegative average 20-day excess returns versus all other observations, or if the interaction term is not negative in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth in the latest available quarterly releases signals that aggregate demand is expanding, raising expected economy-wide cash flows and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available GDPC1 snapshot was 13194.9 billions of chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: Reject if positive latest available GDPC1 growth fails to deliver higher average 60-day S&P 500 returns than nonpositive GDPC1 growth in walk-forward evaluation, or if the sign of the coefficient is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth combined with a falling unemployment rate identifies a self-reinforcing labor recovery that supports consumption and earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days after the macro snapshot updates.
evidence_refs: fact:September 2010 PAYEMS snapshot was 130201 thousand and UNRATE was 9.6 percent
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-up and unemployment-down trigger does not outperform the unconditional 5-day S&P 500 return by at least 5 basis points on average, or if its hit rate is below 50% out of sample.
